exampleSentences: Đại ca đi đâu cũng có vài đứa đệ đi theo sau.
Nó là đệ ruột của tôi, việc gì nó cũng làm được.
Mấy đứa đệ đang ngồi chờ anh ở quán cà phê.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The boss always has a few underlings following him everywhere.
He is my close junior assistant; he can do anything.
A few younger guys are waiting for you at the coffee shop.
notes:
In modern conversational Vietnamese (slang), đệ refers to a younger male who follows or assists an “older brother” (đại ca). It implies a hierarchy of loyalty and protection.

While it still means “disciple” in martial arts or academic contexts, its use in Rank 212 at C1 level focuses on this social hierarchy among friends or groups. Common collocations: đệ ruột (loyal/close underling), mấy đứa đệ (the younger guys/followers).
notes_md:
In modern conversational Vietnamese (slang), **đệ** refers to a younger male who follows or assists an “older brother” (**đại ca**). It implies a hierarchy of loyalty and protection.

While it still means “disciple” in martial arts or academic contexts, its use in Rank 212 at C1 level focuses on this social hierarchy among friends or groups. Common collocations: **đệ ruột** (loyal/close underling), **mấy đứa đệ** (the younger guys/followers).

definitions.en: A younger male follower or junior member in a specific social group.
definitions.vi: Một người em trai hoặc thuộc cấp trong một nhóm xã hội cụ thể.
definitions.es: Un seguidor masculino más joven o miembro junior en un grupo social específico.

example.vi: Nó là đệ ruột của tôi.
example.en: He is my close junior follower.
